confound: The holdout target carried a UNIQUE greppable marker (s18e5), so the agent greps straight to it; the locate-hazard never fired and native+md DID NOT ADOPT md (tool_mix is grep+Read+Edit in all 3 runs, same as native). The -11.9% is within-strategy run variance, not an md effect. The search instance (C-LF-01) had no unique inline anchor (target='Module 118' + 50x-ambiguous value) -> md WAS adopted (outline/section) and won -21.3%.
refined_thesis_N12: md's large-file edge is contingent on (a) a NON-greppable target (locating requires structural navigation grep can't do in one hit) AND (b) the agent actually adopting md. Remove either and native+md collapses to native.
next: a CORRECTED holdout with a non-greppable target (identified by structural position, not a unique inline string) to fire the locate-hazard faithfully; plus the adoption question (does the native+md prompt steer md-use when it helps?).
